title: Posting a notice: pick the right HTTP method

mcq.question_md: Your notice board API follows REST conventions. The frontend sends a new notice — `{title, body, postedBy}` — to `/api/notices` so the server can save it. Which HTTP method should the request use?
mcq.explanation_md: Creating a **new** resource on the server is what **POST** is for — the server assigns the id and typically replies with `201 Created` and the saved notice. The tempting wrong answer is PUT: it also sends a body, but by convention PUT means "replace the resource at this exact URL" — you'd PUT to `/api/notices/12` to update notice 12, not to create one whose id doesn't exist yet. GET is for reading and should never change data (browsers cache and prefetch GETs, so a GET that creates rows will create surprises too), and DELETE removes things.
